sections.overview:
## Overview

Provide a resilient capture-and-settlement flow that deduplicates retried captures and reconciles against the settlement provider daily.
sections.glossary:
## Glossary

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| Capture | Moving an authorised amount to settlement. |
| Idempotency key | Client-supplied token that dedupes a retried capture request. |
| DLQ | Dead-letter queue holding settlement messages that exhausted retries. |
sections.component_architecture:
## Component Architecture Overview

- **Capture API** — accepts capture requests, enforces idempotency.
- **Settlement worker** — drains the settlement queue, writes to the provider.
- **Reconciler** — daily job comparing local ledger to provider statements.

Data flow: Capture API → settlement queue → Settlement worker → provider; Reconciler reads both sides.
sections.definition_of_ready:
## Definition of Ready

- [ ] Provider sandbox credentials available
- [ ] Idempotency-key contract agreed with clients
- [ ] Reconciliation window confirmed (daily, 02:00 UTC)
sections.definition_of_done:
## Definition of Done

- [ ] All child stories meet their acceptance criteria
- [ ] Reconciliation discrepancies alert on-call
- [ ] Dead-letter handling verified in staging

sections.user_story: As a payments client, I want retried captures to be deduplicated, so that a customer is never charged twice.
sections.description:
## Description

The Capture API must treat a repeated request carrying the same idempotency key as a no-op returning the original result.
sections.acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Given a capture with key K succeeded, when the same key K is sent again, then the original capture result is returned and no second charge occurs.
- [ ] Given a new key, when captured, then a fresh capture is created.
sections.dependencies:
## Dependencies

- Idempotency-key contract from the epic DOR.
sections.notes:
## Notes

Evidence: transcripts/session-42.txt (design_decisions — dedupe retried captures).

stories.title: Dead-letter handling for the settlement queue

sections.user_story: As an operator, I want exhausted settlement messages to land in a DLQ, so that failures are visible and recoverable.
sections.description:
## Description

Messages that exhaust retries move to a dead-letter queue with the original payload and failure reason.
sections.acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Given a message fails N retries, when the limit is hit, then it is moved to the DLQ with failure metadata.
- [ ] Given a DLQ message, when replayed, then it re-enters the settlement flow.
sections.dependencies:
## Dependencies

- Depends on "Idempotent capture" so replays are safe.
